brand.name: Revamp
brand.vertical: B2B/professional services — digital marketing agency (SEO, web design, lead generation)

summary: Revamp (by JCE SEO, San Antonio) is a digital marketing agency that sells an integrated, outcome-based retainer it calls a 'Digital Revenue Engine' — combining SEO-optimized web design, local/national SEO, lead generation, PPC, and social media into one system. It serves owner-operated local and regional service businesses (HVAC, roofing, plumbing, law firms, med-spas, restaurants) who are invisible on Google and want predictable, measurable leads rather than commodity SEO. The entry point is a free Google Maps ranking assessment / SEO audit that leads into a strategy call and a monthly retainer.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding — The 90-Day Ramp
campaigns.objective: Activate new retainer clients by setting realistic 90-day SEO expectations, establishing the reporting cadence, and celebrating early wins — directly attacking the brand's #1 churn driver (slow first-90-days results with no communication).
campaigns.trigger: New client signs the retainer / onboarding kickoff scheduled (account created). Send email 1 within seconds of signing.
campaigns.segment: Newly onboarded retainer clients; branch by primary service vertical (home-service contractor, legal/professional, healthcare/med-spa, restaurant/retail) captured at intake.
campaigns.rationale: SEO has an unavoidable 60–90 day ramp, and the intake names 'slow results + no proactive communication' as the top churn risk. Framing the timeline up front and showing a clear progress path is the cheapest way to protect a $1,500–$5,000/mo retainer through its most fragile period.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set the 90-day expectation honestly, explain the reporting cadence, and give them one quick first action so they feel momentum on day one.
emails.subject: Welcome to Revamp — here's exactly what the next 90 days look like
emails.preview: Your first win is one quick step away (and no, you don't have to wait 90 days for it).
keyPoints: Set the honest expectation: real ranking and lead movement shows up in 60–90 days — and explain why that's a feature, not a delay (Google rewards durable authority, not quick tricks)
Lay out the cadence: what we're doing in month 1, when your first report lands, and who their dedicated point of contact is
Give one immediate action (complete the onboarding/access form) so they get a sense of progress on day one
Reassure on the burned-by-past-agencies fear: 'You'll see what we're doing and what it's producing, every step' — reply to a real human anytime

campaigns.name: Free Audit Nurture — From Audit to Booked Call
campaigns.objective: Convert cold free-audit / Google Maps assessment leads into booked strategy calls by quantifying the leads they're losing to competitors and building trust over a multi-email cycle.
campaigns.trigger: Free SEO audit or Google Maps ranking assessment requested/delivered (gated-content download).
campaigns.segment: Audit leads below the sales-ready threshold who haven't booked a call; branch by service vertical and by audit severity (e.g., not in the Maps 3-pack vs. page-2 rankings).
campaigns.rationale: The intake names converting free-audit leads to booked calls as the brand's primary revenue lever, and these leads currently fall into a cold bucket with no follow-up. The audit itself is the perfect personalized hook — it shows the prospect exactly what they're losing.

emails.purpose: Deliver the audit results and frame the real problem behind them — the high-intent local searches happening right now that are going to competitors instead of them.
emails.subject: Your SEO audit is ready — here's who's getting the leads you're not
emails.preview: We found where your competitors are outranking you in San Antonio. The fix is more doable than you think.
keyPoints: Lead with the gap: 'You're not showing up in the Google Maps 3-pack for [service] in [city] — your top 3 competitors are, and that's where ~70% of clicks go'
Translate rankings into revenue: frame missed rankings as missed phone calls and jobs, not abstract SEO metrics
Frame the audit as the diagnosis, the strategy call as the prescription — no obligation, just a plan
Pre-empt the 'burned before' objection: this is a custom look at your business, not a templated report blast

campaigns.name: Audit-to-Retainer Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert prospects who completed the free audit and strategy call into a signed monthly retainer by presenting a custom revenue plan and removing the ROI-risk objection.
campaigns.trigger: Strategy call completed / custom proposal prepared; runs in parallel with a follow-up nudge for prospects who went quiet after the audit.
campaigns.segment: Post-call prospects with a custom proposal pending; branch by hot (engaged on call) vs. stalled (no reply after proposal).
campaigns.rationale: Days-to-weeks sales cycle closes on a proposal after the strategy call — but there's no automated push to close or re-engage the ghosters. This is the email that turns a free audit into recurring revenue.

emails.purpose: Deliver the custom Digital Revenue Engine proposal, make the ROI math concrete, and dismantle the 'I got burned last time' objection with proof and a clear, low-risk next step.
emails.subject: Your custom growth plan is inside — built from your audit numbers
emails.preview: What ranking page 1 for your top keywords is actually worth in monthly revenue.
emails.cta: Approve your plan & lock in your start date
keyPoints: Tie the proposal back to their audit: 'Based on the searches we found in your market, here's the lead and revenue picture once you're ranking'
Handle the top objection head-on: how Revamp's reporting and transparent milestones differ from the agency that took their money and showed nothing
Show proof — a comparable local client's before/after (rankings → leads) in the same kind of vertical
Lower the risk: clear month-by-month deliverables, a real point of contact, and an easy way to ask questions before signing

campaigns.name: Strategy Call Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended strategy calls and recover no-shows so audit-stage leads actually make it to the conversation that closes them.
campaigns.trigger: Strategy/discovery call booked; upcoming-appointment reminders; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled strategy or discovery call.
campaigns.rationale: The entire conversion motion hinges on the booked strategy call — every no-show is a lost retainer. Reminders cut no-shows by ~35% on average, making this the fastest way to protect pipeline that's already been earned.

emails.purpose: The 24-hour-before reminder — confirm the call, prime them with what we'll cover, and make rescheduling frictionless so a conflict becomes a reschedule, not a no-show.
emails.subject: See you tomorrow — your Revamp strategy call at [time]
emails.preview: We'll walk through your audit and the exact plan to get your phone ringing. Need to move it? One click.
emails.cta: Confirm or reschedule in one click
keyPoints: Confirm date, time, and join/dial-in details with an add-to-calendar link
Set the agenda so it feels worth showing up: 'We'll cover your audit findings, where your competitors are winning, and a clear 90-day plan'
Make rescheduling one click — a conflict should reschedule, never ghost
Tell them what to bring: their current marketing spend and their #1 service they want more of

campaigns.name: Win-Back — Cold Audit Leads & Churned Clients
campaigns.objective: Reactivate dormant free-audit leads and churned retainer clients with fresh proof of value, and sunset non-responders to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold (90+ days no opens/clicks/booking) or client cancellation.
campaigns.segment: Dormant audit leads who never booked; separately, churned clients (different message referencing what changed and what they last achieved).
campaigns.rationale: Old audit leads and churned clients currently get zero re-engagement. Their market position has likely slipped further since they went quiet — a fresh 'here's where you stand now' hook recovers 5–15% at almost no cost.

emails.purpose: Re-open the conversation by reminding dormant leads what they're still losing and offering a fresh, no-strings look at their current rankings.
emails.subject: Still invisible on Google? Let's check where you stand now
emails.preview: A lot can change in 90 days — usually not in your favor. Here's a fresh look, free.
emails.cta: Get an updated free ranking check
keyPoints: Pattern-interrupt: 'It's been a while — and your competitors haven't slowed down. Want to see where you rank today?'
Offer a fresh, updated audit/ranking check as a low-friction reason to re-engage (new data, not a repeat of the old report)
For churned clients, reference the results they last saw and what's new at Revamp since (new services, reporting, packages)
Keep it light and no-pressure — the goal is a single click, with a clean unsubscribe for those who are truly done
